title: The One Without The Ski Trip

lines.text: Central Perk, Chandler and Joey are sitting on the couch reading.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Can I see the comics?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This is the New York Times.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, may I see the comics?
[Monica and Phoebe enter.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Guys. I thought you were taking Ross to the game?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We are. He's meeting us here.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! Rachel is meeting us here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh come on, they can be in the same room.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, you shoulda been there last night.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why? What happened now?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well Ross was hangin' out over at our place, Rachel comes over to borrow some moisturiser from Chandler....

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah y-you, how hard is it to say something? Rachel came over to borrow something.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Anyway! Her and Ross just started yelling at each other.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait. Why was he yelling at her? He's the one who slept with someone else.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I guess he says that because they were on a break when it happened, that she should of forgiven him by now.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Whoa!! He is soo unreasonable! God, although I think I understand what he means. Oh my God, this is like 60 Minutes, okay, when, when, at first you're really mad at that pharmaceutical company for making the drug and then y'know you just feel bad for the people because they needed to make their hair grow.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what this is like? This is like when my parents got divorced. Man, I hope Ross doesn't try to kidnap me after Cub Scouts.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know I had a dream where Ross and Rachel were still together, they never broke up. And we were all just like hanging out, and everyone was happy....

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I had the same dream!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, and nobody slept with that Xerox girl.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I had the opposite dream.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what maybe it's gonna be okay, I mean it's been a week.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I mean it's never taken me a week to get over a relationship.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's never taken you more than a shower to get over a relationship.
[Rachel enters.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (seeing her) Okay, let's go!! Let's hit the road!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Let's get the show on it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, let me just get a cup of coffee.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh Rachel, I know the best coffee house and it's sooo close.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Closer than here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (turning around and picking a cup off of a table) Oh, hey, look, I found coffee! (handing her the cup) Okay, let's skedaddle.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait, I'm not just gonna drink somebody's old coffee.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, your highness.
[Ross enters behind Rachel, and look at each other for a moment.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (in a deep voice, imitating Ross) Um, Rachel I'm really sorry. (imitating Rachel) That's okay, do you wanna get back together? (imitating Ross) Yeah, okay. (in her normal voice) Did anyone else hear that?!
Opening Credits

Chandler and Joey's, the whole gang is there, except for Ross and Rachel. Joey is trying to eat Chinese with chopsticks and fails miserably. There's a knock on the door, and Chandler answers it to reveal Rachel

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (softly) Is he here?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. (smiles) Here's your moisturiser. Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica and Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You guys are gonna love meee! Okay, check it out, Thursday night, five tickets, Calvin Klein lingerie show, and you guys are coming with me. (they're all silent and look away) Okay, I said that out loud right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, yes, it's just that we ah, we kinda all ready, made plans with Ross.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, well okay. Well, there you go.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, it's just that he got this new like home theater dealie, and he wants y'know, us to check it out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hm-mm.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, he's really excited about it too, he even recorded show times on his answering machine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We're sorry honey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, it's okay. (starts to leave)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Rach, it's, it's ah, it's not that we don't want to, really. (quietly) Are we talking models in their underwear?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And heels.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (He turns around to Chandler looking for approval to go with Rachel, Chandler mouths 'Come on!') (turning back to Rachel) Ross, did ask us first, and we set that night aside.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, hey, come on, if he asked you first, that's only fair. (leaves)
[Chandler makes a noise of absolute disgust and heads into the living room.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohhh boy, do I feel bad.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Very bad.
[We see Chandler lighting up a cigarette.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Chandler what are you doing?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (jumps back and points at the cigarette) Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You're smoking again?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, actually, yesterday I was smoking again. Today, I'm, I'm smoking still.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why would you start again after chewing all that quitting gum?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, I'm telling you this is just like my parents divorce, which is when I started smoking in the first place.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Weren't you nine?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeahhh. I'm tellin' ya something, that ah, first smoke after nap time....
[There's a knock on the door.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh that's great, with my luck, that's gonna be him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Him? Him, Ross?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nope, hymn 253, His Eyes Are On The Sparrow! When my parents got divorced is when I started using humour as a defence mechanism. (answers the door and it's Rachel again.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! Uhh, do you guys have plans for the weekend? Because I have my sister on hold, and she said that we could use her cabin for the weekend and go skiing. Huh? I'm asking you first, right?!. I mean I'm playing by the rules.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Absolutely, yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler! You're smoking? What are you doing?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, shut up!! You're not my real Mom!!
Ross's, the gang, minus Rachel of course, is there. Chandler is forced to smoke by an open window.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (obviously cold) Hey, can you close that window Chandler? My nipples can cut glass over here!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait. Really?! 'Cause mine get me out of tickets.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, you guys I just wanna say, I really, really appreciate you spending this time with me. It's been a pretty hard time right now, so I just wanna say thanks.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Can somebody else hug him? I have to stay by the window.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh hey, hey, huh, how about this weekend we have a laser disc marathon okay, and maybe a tournament on my new dart board? Huh, huh, what do you think? (in an Irish accent) Two days of darts, it'll be great!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It'll be great for next weekend.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no, no, this weekend guys!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It'll be great for next weekend. I mean, (in an Irish accent) it'll be grrreat.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, we were um, sorta invited to go skiing, y'know Rachel's sister's cabin. (Chandler goes back to the window to smoke again.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, for the whole weekend?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We're really sorry, but um, she did ask us first.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, that's okay, I mean if you guys all have to go away for the first weekend I'm alone by myself, y'know then I totally, totally understand.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know what, I can stay, I'm gonna stay. 'Cause the last time I went skiing I was to afraid to jump off the chair lift, I just went around and around.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, Pheebs we kinda need you to drive us all up there in your grandmother's cab, but y'know what, I'll stay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Noo! I'll stay. He's my brother.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What a pity stay?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! We're gonna have fun. We can make fudge!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pity food? Y'know what that's okay, all right, I don't need any of you to stay, okay nobody stays.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, then, I might as well offer to stay.
In Phoebe's Grandmother's cab, driving up to the cabin. Phoebe's driving, Rachel's sitting shotgun, Chandler, Monica, and Joey are in the back seat.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Chandler and Monica) Hey, does anybody else feel bad about Ross?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why? Do you think he's still mad at us?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Joey) Well he's probably mad after you called him this morning to borrow his goggles.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? Mine aren't tinted.
[Chandler starts to light a cigarette.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Chandler!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What does the sign say?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Beam me up Jesus.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, the 'No Smoking' sign. There's no smoking in my Grandmother's cab.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, well, then, I-I have to go to the bathroom.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Please!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No Chandler no! No unscheduled stops. You can go when we stop for gas.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, come on, there's a rest stop right up there! Come on, I really have to goooooooooo.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, now I have to go!!
The rest stop, Phoebe's pulling in.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Here we go. Okay, brace yourselves.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?
[Both Chandler and Joey put their feet up against the glass, Monica doesn't and gets thrown up against the glass.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ow!
[Joey gets out and sprints to the bathroom and Chandler follows with a cigarette in his hands.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Rachel, who's staying in the cab.) Aren't you gonna go?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (getting out) No, Rachel never pees in public restrooms.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, they never have any paper in there y'know. So my rule is 'no tissue, no tuschy.' (Phoebe laughs and gets out.) Well, if everybody's going. (She gets out and starts to close the door.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, y'know what don't close it (Rachel slams the door shut locking themselves out.) 'cause the... keys...are in there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, no-no-no-no-no-no!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Joey) My lighter's in there! (points to the cab)
Commercial Break
The rest stop, the gang is still stuck, Chandler is kneeling at the rear bumper.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Damn! (stands up) The tailpipe's not hot enough to light this!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Relax okay, I-I-I can get this open. Anybody have a coat hanger?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh I do! Op, no, wait a minute, I took it out of my shirt when I put it on this morning.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, if you're parents hadn't got divorced, you'd be able to answer a question like a normal person?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, I just need a wire something to jimmy it. Oh hey, one of you guys give me the underwire from your bra!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on! Who has the biggest boobs?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Please!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoever has the biggest boobs, has the biggest bra, therefore has the biggest wire.

lines.speaker: The Girls
lines.text: No, not getting my bra!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: If you wanna get back in the car, we need the wire, your call.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, Monica's are the biggest.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: These tiny, little non-breasts?! Please, it's gotta be Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What, no, no, no, mine are deceptively small I mean, I-I-I actually sometimes, st-stuff my bra.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right then, your bra would still be big.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I stuff outside the bra.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ladies, ladies, let's just compromise okay? Phoebe, Rachel take off Monica's bra.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, forget it, nevermind, you can have mine.
[Chandler crumples up his cigarette pack and throws it on the ground.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (seeing him) Chandler, what are you doing? There is a trash can right there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I thought if I littered, that crying Indian might come by and save us.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (finishing removing her bra) Okay, there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thank you Phoebe, that is very, very generous.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, now let's decide who has the nicest ass.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (opening the door) And there you go!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh, yeahhhhh!!!
[They all run to get in the cab, and Chandler pulls out a smoke.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: At least let me smoke it to the good part.
[Phoebe puts the car in gear and starts to back out.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (The car moves a few feet and sputters to a stop.) Oh, no!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What, what's it, what's going on?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, this has happened before.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So you know how to fix it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yep. Put more gas in.
Carol and Susan's, Carol is setting a romantic dinner for Susan as there is a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: (answering the door) Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Hey, what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, oh just ah, I was just wondering, when you and I split up, did you get the tape that was half the last episode of M*A*S*H and half the hostages coming home?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Ah yeah, but now it's Susan and me in Mexico and the hostages coming home.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Where's Ben?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: He's sleeping.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ahh. (notices the table) Ooh, is this a ah, is this a bad time?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Umm, yeah, actually, Susan's gonna be home any minute, it's kinda an anniversary.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! I thought you guys got married in uh, January?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: It's not that kind of anniversary.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah! (realises) Oh.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Sooo!! Anyway...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm, candles, champagne, yeah anniversaries are great. 'Cause you know love lasts forever, y'know. Nothing like it in this lifetime, money in the bank, so Rachel and I broke up.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Oh God, Ross I am so sorry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, well.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Y'know what, I want to talk to you about this so much, but we should probably do it when we could really get into it, are you free for dinner tomorrow night?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah, I'd love that.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Oh, great! Me too.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I guess it all started when Rachel got this new job. (he sits down at the table.)
The rest stop, Phoebe is on the phone to the motor club.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, yeah. (to Monica and Rachel) Triple A can pick us up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, what town are we near?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Freemont. West-Westmont, ah Westburg?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Monica) Then why are you answering? Do you at least know what route we're on?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, we are definitely on Route 27.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (on phone) We are at a rest stop on Route 27. Okay. (to Rachel) There is no Route 27. (listens) (to Rachel) Okay, either 93 or 76?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know, I'm sorry, I always slept in the back when we drove up here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (on phone) Hey, can you send somebody up and down 76 and check every rest stop, and, and also 93? (listens) Okay! (hangs up) Yeah, no they don't do that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh, okay, well somebody will come and save us.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Who? I mean have you seen a car come by here in the last hour and a half? I think we should call Ross, maybe he can get a car and come pick us up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! No, I am not getting in a car with Ross, we will just have to live here!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But if...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No you guys, I am not getting in a car with him, you'll have to think of something else.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh good, oh Joey and Chandler are back.
[Joey walks up helping Chandler.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So the going for help went well?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah, Smokey Joe here got half way to the highway and collapsed.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I have the lung capacity of a two year old. (starts to light another cigarette.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Then why are you smoking?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well it's very unsettling.
Carol and Susan's, Ross is eating the dinner Carol made for Susan.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ....right? Right? I mean it's pretty unbelievable y'know, I mean they just took off, took off without even looking back. Y'know I don't, I don't need them, huh, I've got you guys now as friends, you and Susan.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Ah, Susan will be so pleased.
[Ross's beeper goes off.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (looking at the page) 717? (to Carol) Where's 717? (He gets up to return the page, Carol starts to take the last of the food into the kitchen, but Ross grabs the last piece.) Hey, you've have more of these for Susan right?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: No. But it's okay, I'll just put out pickles or something.
[Cut to the rest stop, Monica and Phoebe are waiting anxiously by the phone as it rings.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (answering the phone) (whispering) Ross, thank God.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pheebs? What, why are you whispering?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I ate a bug.
[Rachel starts to walk up.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (running over to stop Rachel) Hey Rach, the tampons here are only a penny. Let's stock up. (takes her into the bathroom)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Listen Ross, we ran out of gas, and we don't know where we are, so we can't get a tow truck.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, now you want a favour?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, please.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, oh, I'm sorry your car broke down Pheebs, but I'm a little too busy with some of my real friends right now, but please call to let me know you got home safely okay?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: (running over and grabbing the phone away from Ross) (on phone) Phoebe, hang on a second. (Hands Ross her keys) Here, take my car, go pick up your friends.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I'm not gonna pick them up.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Listen, we both know you're gonna do it 'cause you're not a jerk. Okay? So you can either sulk here for a half hour and then go pick them up, or save us both time and sulk in the car.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, Rachel doesn't want me to....

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Look, I-I-I am sorry that Rachel dumped you 'cause she fell in love with that Mark guy, and you are the innocent victim in all of this, but don't punish your friends for what Rachel did to you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, you're right.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: (on phone) Phoebe hang on a second Ross wants to say something. (listens) What? (listens) (to Ross) You slept with someone else?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We were on a break!!! Okay!! (grabs the phone) We were, we were..., (calms down) yeah. Where are you? I'll find you. (hangs up)

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: You slept with another woman?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, you-you're-you're one to talk.
The rest stop, Joey is making a sign.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, done.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (reading the sign) What's 'pleh'?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's 'help' spelled backwards so that the helicopters can read it from the air!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Huh. What's doofus spelled backwards?
[Ross drives up.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (all excited) Op, op, car! Car!! (sees it's Ross) Ugh!!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it's Ross on one of his drives!

lines.speaker: Chandler and Joey
lines.text: Hey!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What is he doing here?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: He is saving your butt, ah, unless of course I'm stepping on some toes here, in which case I can just mosey on, I've got plenty of people to help on the Interstate.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: No! Come on!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right!! Fine! Fine.
[Ross grabs the gas can he brought along, and walks through Joey's sign destroying it.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Arrrghh!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no, now it's not gonna make any sense!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Chandler and Monica) You guys, what, what do we do about Ross who drove all the way up here? What do we do? Just like send him back and we're then gonna go skiing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, this is horrible, it's just horrible.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Guys, do you think we should ask Ross to come along?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know, what about Rachel? I mean how are we even gonna ask her?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ask me what?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, if ah, it might be okay if Ross came skiing?

lines.speaker: Chandler, Phoebe, and Joey
lines.text: No, I wasn't gonna ask you that, no.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You guys are unbelievable. No! He cannot come.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's horrible.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (sarcastic) Oh please, can't I come to your special, magical cabin?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why would you even want to come Ross? You're a horrible skier.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh-oh, hitting me where it hurts, my ski skills.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Here we go again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I-I can't handle this, you guys.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what, I can handle it, handle's my middle name. Actually it's the ah, middle part of my first name.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right Pheebs, your cab's ready.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, let's go!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're welcome.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry, were you speaking to me or sleeping with someone else?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We were on a break!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know Ross why don't you put that on your answering machine!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey-hey, it's valid okay? And I'm not the only one who thinks so, Monica agrees with me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Monica) What?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (shyly) I don't know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's what you said last night.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What I said was, was that I understood. Joey's the one who agreed with you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really Joey?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (pause) What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know what, but there is, there is no right or wrong, here.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I think it's very obvious who's wrong here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Obviously not to Joey.
[They all turn around and look at Joey.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (pause) What?
[They all start fighting with each other.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Rachel) Look both, Joey and Monica feel the same way that I do. No-no-no-no.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Guys, guys, guess who I am?! (starts dancing around in an effort to stop the fighting.)
[They stop briefly to look at Chandler, but then start fighting again.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! Hey! Hey! Hey!! Hey!!! (they all stop fighting, Chandler continues to dance.) Look what you're doing to Chandler!! (Chandler finally stops) (to Ross and Rachel) Yeah, look, we know this is really, really hard for you guys. Okay? (Ross starts to leave) You don't, all right you don't have to love each other, okay? You don't, you don't even have to like each other much right now. But please, you have to figure out a way to be around each other.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, and not put us in the middle.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, otherwise, I mean that's, that's, that's just it for us hanging out together. Y'know is that what you want? (they both look away) Can you be civil?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Good, all right, let's get back in the car, 'cause it's freezing, and my chest is unsupported.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh what, wait, wait a second, I mean, what are we doing? Who's going with who?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, you guys, you guys should go. (Joey tries to say something, but Ross cuts him off.) No, I'm, you, you planned this all out, and I don't want to ruin it, so you guys should just go.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on man, you drove all the way up here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no, really, I've got to take the car back anyway, I'm spending all day tomorrow with Ben, It's fine, no guilt I promise.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, we'll call you when we get back.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Maybe we can like go to a movie or something.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Or, or the rodeo!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That would be great.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I was being Shelly Winters from The Poseidon Adventure.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know!
[They all get in the cab and drive away.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Bye!!!
[Ross tries to start the truck, and discovers the battery's dead.]
Closing Credits
Carol and Susan's, Carol is running to answer the door.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: (looking through the peephole) Ugh. (opening the door) Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hi! Sorry I'm late. Were you sleeping?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Ahh, nooo!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, great! Listen, oh I had to get you a whole new battery. I got you the best one I could, 'cause that's not where you want to skimp.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: You're a genius, Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, well it came to about $112, but what the hell, just call it an even 110?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Okay, I'll pay you tomorrow. (pushes him out the door)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Okay, bye!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So they ah, they all took off, it was pretty hard watching them go, y'know?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Yeah, okay, bye. (closes the door, turns out the lights, and runs back to the bedroom)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (outside the door) So I'm gonna take off then!
End
